original_hypothesis.title: Owner can accidentally brick privileged operations by renouncing ownership, preventing manager/accountant/feeWallet updates needed for liveness
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: HypoVault relies on onlyOwner setters (setManager, setAccountant, setFeeWallet) to remediate operational issues (e.g., feeWallet unset, accountant misconfigured, manager key rotation). If owner renounces ownership, these controls are irreversibly lost, potentially making the vault unable to recover from misconfiguration or external dependency failures.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a realistic operational failure with permanent consequences. Given the system’s reliance on privileged roles for epoch progress and configuration, losing ownership can lock users into a non-recoverable state if any parameter needs adjustment.
deployment_assumptions: Owner calls renounceOwnership() at some point (by mistake or governance decision)
At least one future need arises to update manager/accountant/feeWallet for liveness or safety
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Ownable is inherited; renounceOwnership() is exposed by OZ Ownable. No deployment/ops controls are provided to show it is restricted in practice.
preconditions: Owner address exists and has ability to call renounceOwnership()
Vault requires future configuration changes to maintain liveness (feeWallet, manager, accountant) or to respond to incidents

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: no on-chain safeguard against renouncing while critical parameters are unset
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No override disabling renounceOwnership or enforcing safe preconditions

analysis_questions: Is renounceOwnership intended/allowed per protocol ops?
Are there any emergency paths for users if owner is gone and manager/accountant become unusable?
Does accountant contract ownership similarly affect vault liveness (updatePoolsHash/lockVault governance)?
validation_requirements: Review operational docs/deployment scripts for ownership management
Simulate scenario: feeWallet unset + owner renounced; confirm profitable withdrawals cannot execute
Assess whether new manager can be installed without owner (it cannot)

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Not an external attacker-driven exploit under the stated trust model; a mistaken renounce locks the system into its current configuration permanently, making later remediation impossible.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Potential permanent user fund lock if misconfiguration occurs after renounce
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: None on-chain; requires migration via manager manage() if still functional and trusted

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No tests/documented safeguards around renounceOwnership in a live vault context

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Monitor ownership changes; alert on owner becoming address(0)

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
